data.title: pixel perimeter
data.solution:
import sys

w, h = map(int, input().split())

# Handle edge cases where w or h is 1 (no hole)
if w < 2 or h < 2:
    print(w * h)
else:
    # 2*w + 2*h - 4 accounts for corners to avoid double-counting
    print(2 * w + 2 * h - 4)
data.statement:
Goal:
Calculate the perimeter of a solid rectangle made of "pixels" (characters) on a grid. You are given the width and height of the rectangle, but some "noise" characters are placed around it.
<<Example>>

```
ooooo   Width: 5
o---o   Height: 3
ooooo   Characters: 12
```
data.stubGenerator:
read w:int h:int
write int output
data.inputDescription:
Line 1: Two integers [[W]] and [[H]] (width and height of the rectangle).
Line 2: A character [[C]] used to draw the rectangle.
Next H lines: A string of length [[W]] representing each row of the rectangle.
data.outputDescription: A single integer representing the number of characters [[C]] that are on the edge of the rectangle (the perimeter).

lastVersion.statementHTML:
Goal

Goal:
Calculate the perimeter of a solid rectangle made of "pixels" (characters) on a grid. You are given the width and height of the rectangle, but some "noise" characters are placed around it.
Example


ooooo   Width: 5
o---o   Height: 3
ooooo   Characters: 12





   

      
Input

      
Line 1: Two integers W and H (width and height of the rectangle).
Line 2: A character C used to draw the rectangle.
Next H lines: A string of length W representing each row of the rectangle.

   

   

      
Output

      
A single integer representing the number of characters C that are on the edge of the rectangle (the perimeter).

   

   

      
Constraints

      
1<=W,H<=100
W<H

   

   

      
Example

      

         

            
Input

            
5 3

         

         

            
Output

            
12
